title: pseudowintera axillaris
intro: Pseudowintera axillaris, also known as Horopito, is a native New Zealand plant that belongs to the Winteraceae family. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional Māori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Horopito include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Horopito can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
